assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Semiconductor and AI capex remains the cleanest funded structural growth lane.
assessed_lanes.why_now: AI infrastructure leadership remains persistent, but position-size discipline matters.
assessed_lanes.macro_policy_reason: Regime and price leadership still support AI compute exposure, but concentration discipline applies.
assessed_lanes.freshness_note: Historical relative strength was available and included in scoring.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: AI infrastructure remains supported by capex and leadership persistence, but position-size discipline matters.

assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Cyber spend is linked to AI, cloud, data-center and geopolitical resilience.
assessed_lanes.why_now: Offers digital-infrastructure exposure with less direct semiconductor cyclicality.
assessed_lanes.freshness_note: Historical relative strength was available and included in scoring.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: AI infrastructure remains supported by capex and leadership persistence, but position-size discipline matters.

assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Power demand, grid bottlenecks and reshoring capex support infrastructure spend.
assessed_lanes.why_now: PAVE remains useful, but GRID is the cleaner thematic challenger.
assessed_lanes.macro_policy_reason: AI power demand and infrastructure policy support the grid lane; funding still depends on PAVE-vs-GRID duel evidence.
assessed_lanes.freshness_note: Historical relative strength was available and included in scoring.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: Power demand, grid bottlenecks and reshoring capex remain supportive.

assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Copper transmits grid, AI power and electrification demand into commodity equities.
assessed_lanes.why_now: Becomes more fundable if copper momentum and China demand confirm.
assessed_lanes.freshness_note: Historical relative strength was available and included in scoring.
assessed_lanes.non_promotion_reason: Not promoted because it still trails SPY on 3-month relative strength.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: Copper and critical minerals are linked to grid, AI power and electrification, but need commodity momentum.

assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Automation capex can broaden beyond semiconductors into industrial productivity.
assessed_lanes.why_now: Needs clearer industrial order momentum.
assessed_lanes.freshness_note: Historical relative strength was available and included in scoring.
assessed_lanes.non_promotion_reason: Structural case is credible, but timing confirmation still trails higher-ranked lanes.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: Automation remains structural but overlaps with AI hardware and needs industrial breadth.
assessed_lanes.direct_rs_vs_holding_reason: Semiconductor leadership can be challenged only by cleaner or broader AI implementation if direct strength is compelling.

assessed_lanes.evidence_summary: Critical mineral supply chains matter for electrification, defense and grid capex.

assessed_lanes.non_promotion_reason: Not promoted because it still trails SPY on 3-month relative strength.
assessed_lanes.macro_freshness_note: Copper and critical minerals are linked to grid, AI power and electrification, but need commodity momentum.

assessed_lanes.direct_rs_vs_holding_reason: Hedge sleeve can be challenged by broader commodities or cash-like ballast if gold implementation keeps underperforming.

authority.decision_framework: Legacy macro lane adjustments remain available for backward-compatible lane discovery only.
authority.input_state_contract: Pricing audit and relative-strength artifacts remain production inputs; macro audit is metadata-only until promoted.
authority.operational_runbook: Validate schema and compatibility before lane discovery; later phases may replace legacy regime logic after fixture/shadow review.
authority.output_contract: Client-facing macro content remains governed by the existing runtime report path and must not expose shadow-only fields.

boe.etf_implication: No direct portfolio action unless UK exposure enters the universe.
boe.main_risk: Sticky services inflation keeps UK duration and cyclicals mixed.

boj.etf_implication: Avoid treating long-duration exposure as automatically defensive.
boj.main_risk: A yield or yen shock can tighten global financial conditions.

ecb.etf_implication: Non-U.S. developed exposure remains watchlist, not automatic add.
ecb.likely_direction: Gradual easing bias if inflation and growth allow
ecb.main_risk: Europe may not outperform without earnings breadth and currency support.

fed.etf_implication: Prefer quality, profitable growth and cash discipline over weak balance-sheet beta.
fed.likely_direction: Hold-to-ease path, but timing remains data-dependent
fed.main_risk: Real-rate repricing or delayed easing can pressure duration and speculative beta.

pboc.etf_implication: China exposure remains tactical watchlist unless price confirmation improves.
pboc.likely_direction: Targeted support rather than broad reflation unless confidence improves
pboc.main_risk: China stimulus disappointment can weigh on China beta and commodities.

notes: This is a compatibility decomposition for the legacy proxy regime path.
Deterministic derived confidence is planned for Phase 3 and is not yet production authority.
Current legacy regime label: Risk-on growth

cross_asset_confirmation: Semiconductor leadership supports SMH, but SPY overlap must remain explicit.
Small-cap and duration signals are not strong enough to justify broad beta expansion.
Gold is treated as a hedge review item unless price behavior improves.

AI compute infrastructure.reason: Regime and price leadership still support AI compute exposure, but concentration discipline applies.

China platform beta.reason: Policy support is not yet enough to offset confidence and price-confirmation risk.

Defense innovation / sovereign resilienc.reason: Policy support is durable, but implementation quality remains the main decision bottleneck.

Gold hedge review.reason: Gold must prove hedge behavior; macro status alone is not enough for fresh capital.

Grid buildout / electrification.reason: AI power demand and infrastructure policy support the grid lane; funding still depends on PAVE-vs-GRID duel evidence.

Non-U.S. developed diversification.reason: Diversification gap is real, but policy and relative-strength confirmation are not yet decisive.

Rate-sensitive small caps.reason: Current regime does not yet confirm broad small-cap risk appetite.

policy_catalysts.latest_signal: Capital spending and strategic supply-chain policy continue to support semiconductor and infrastructure lanes.
policy_catalysts.policy_area: AI infrastructure and semiconductor supply chains

policy_catalysts.latest_signal: Defense-budget durability remains a structural support, but ETF vehicle choice still matters.
Energy-security policy keeps uranium and nuclear infrastructure relevant, but timing remains price-confirmation dependent.
Support remains watchlist-relevant, but confidence and earnings confirmation are still required.

portfolio_implications: Risk appetite is supportive, but fresh adds still need position-size room and pricing confirmation.
Growth and infrastructure lanes can be considered if they do not worsen concentration.
Defensive hedges should be reviewed for opportunity cost.

what_changed: AI / semiconductor leadership remains the dominant equity impulse.
Gold hedge behavior remains under review rather than automatic ballast.

regime_memory.decision_rule: Do not rotate aggressively unless a regime shift persists for at least two runs or cross-asset confirmation becomes broad.

report_transfer.summary: Risk-on growth has persisted for 2 run(s); transition state is stable, breadth is mixed, and cross-asset confirmation is mixed.

report_transfer.style_rule: Transfer only decision-relevant macro information. Do not dump the full research pack into the report.
